reasoning: The Federal Reserve is currently in an easing/hold pattern, and banks have no macro incentive to raise deposit rates. The national savings rate (SNDR) steadily peaked in late 2024 at 0.46% and has dropped smoothly to 0.41%, where it has remained flat for the first four months of 2025. With rate cuts still expected (or at worst, holds), it's highly unlikely that broad-based bank increases will push this weighted average up in May. While some unpredictable noise is possible, this is a very smooth aggregate metric. Uncertainty increases for the July and October horizons as inflation could rebound forcing un-priced hikes, hence drawing those probabilities slightly closer to 0.5 as instructed.
